claim: SAS-Cert is currently supported as a diagnostic reliability certificate for EEG augmentation candidates
evidence_summary: component-gated v1 AUC is 0.8395 and artifact-gate physio AUC is 0.9022 on both CBraMod and ST

claim: The old scalar SAS score is directionally wrong on the PhysioNetMI mixed-bad pool
evidence_summary: current scalar SAS AUC is 0.1969 on CBraMod and 0.1662 on ST

claim: Artifact-gate physio is the strongest diagnostic variant in the current pack
evidence_summary: artifact-gate physio AUC is 0.9022 on both backbones, higher than component-gated v1 0.8395
allowed_wording: strongest diagnostic variant in this pack

claim: Current weighting or rejection policies are not reliable deployable training methods
evidence_summary: CBraMod repaired weighting improves Macro-F1 but worsens ECE; ST SoftWeight improves mean Macro-F1 but fails subject/seed reliability

claim: CBraMod repaired artifact-gate physio produces classification gains but fails calibration
ST SoftWeight no-reject gives a positive mean effect but fails reliability
evidence_summary: Macro-F1 +0.64pp, majority-seed subject win rate 0.15, seed win rate 0.00

claim: Candidate-only fold summaries do not explain ST SoftWeight benefit
evidence_summary: max candidate-only Spearman is 0.1168, below 0.35 threshold

claim: Protocol leakage was not detected in the diagnostic pack
evidence_summary: protocol gate passed; target heldout not used for score or threshold selection in audits
allowed_wording: no protocol leakage detected in audited outputs

claim: SAS-Cert improves few-shot accuracy reliably across subjects and seeds
evidence_summary: ST and CBraMod training policies were explicitly not promoted

claim: SAS-Cert should be presented as an augmentation-selection method ready for deployment
evidence_summary: weighting-policy non-promotion gate passed, meaning non-promotion is part of the final decision
